governing_principle: All claims must be classified before publication. Unclassified claims may not be published.

doctrine_claim.description: A project-governing statement derived directly from the SuperSXO asset thesis. Does not require external citation.
doctrine_claim.review_requirement: Must be verified for alignment with the governing statement in SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE.md.
doctrine_claim.example: SEO brings the visitor. SXO governs what happens next.

general_industry_claim.description: A broadly accepted industry statement that reflects common knowledge within the SEO, UX, or CRO disciplines. Use with caution as consensus may shift.
general_industry_claim.review_requirement: Must be reviewed for current accuracy. Flagged if the claim is contested or has become outdated.
general_industry_claim.example: Page speed is a ranking factor for Google Search.

source_backed_claim.description: A factual or externally verifiable claim that must be supported by a cited and verifiable source before publication.
source_backed_claim.review_requirement: Source must be identified, linked, and confirmed as credible before the content is published.
source_backed_claim.example: According to [source], X% of users abandon a page after Y seconds of load time.

interpretation.description: A strategic reading or analytical position held by SuperSXO. Not presented as universal fact but as the project's reasoned perspective.
interpretation.review_requirement: Must be clearly framed as interpretation. Must not be presented as a factual claim without evidence.
interpretation.example: AI-generated search results represent a structural shift in how search experience must be governed.

commercial_claim.description: A claim related to offers, products, diagnostic reports, or monetization features of the SuperSXO asset.
commercial_claim.review_requirement: Must be reviewed against MONETIZATION_BOUNDARY.md. Must not contain unsupported performance promises. Must pass the monetization gate.
commercial_claim.example: The SuperSXO Score diagnostic identifies gaps in your search-to-action journey.

blocked_claim.description: A prohibited or unsupported claim. May not be published in any form under any circumstances.
blocked_claim.review_requirement: Must be documented in DECISION_LOG.md if encountered during content review. Cannot be reclassified without owner approval.
blocked_claim.example: SuperSXO guarantees higher rankings or conversion growth.
